clusters.title: Son charged after the alleged fatal stabbing of his father in Sydney's west
clusters.neutral_headline: Son Charged With Fatal Stabbing Of Father

items.title: Son charged after the alleged fatal stabbing of his father in Sydney's west
items.standfirst: A man has been charged after allegedly fatally stabbing his father in Sydney's west last night.
items.content: A man has been charged after allegedly fatally stabbing his father in Sydney's west last night.

items.title: Son charged after father allegedly fatally stabbed in Sydney's west
items.standfirst: Videos posted to social media showed more than a dozen police cars, ambulances and the police helicopter responding to the incident.
items.content: A man has been charged after his father was allegedly fatally stabbed in Sydney's west last night. Emergency services were called to Clouta Place, Emu Plains at 6.45pm yesterday.Videos posted to social media showed more than a dozen police cars, ambulances and the police helicopter responding to the incident. READ MORE: What six more months of war would mean for AustraliaNSW Police said officers found the 58-year-old father with multiple stab wounds to his upper body.He was treated by NSW Ambulance paramedics but died at the scene.A 30-year-old man, who was later identified as the victim's son, was arrested at the scene and taken to Penrith police station.He has been charged with one count of domestic violence murder, police said this morning.He was refused bail and will appear in court today. READ MORE: 'We must look after ourselves': PM hit with three key demands Police said there was a history of domestic violence allegations, however, there were no reports in the past few years.Residents told 9News that the family have been living at the home for more than 40 years. "[It is] a quiet street, you would never think something like this would occur here," a resident said."There were no indications that something like this would occur up there." "I can't say I would have expected something like this."Support is available from the National Sexual Assault, Domestic and Family Violence Counselling Service at 1800RESPECT (1800 737 732)NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
